“Time is measured by the revolutions of whatever planet or other heavenly body is involved. With reference to ‘celestial time,’ the Lord said to Abraham, ‘that Kolob was after the manner of the Lord, according to its times and seasons in the revolutions thereof; that one revolution was a day unto the Lord, after his manner of reckoning, it being one thousand years according to the time appointed…

The reckoning of . . . time. Time is a relative measurement of duration that enables us to think and speak in terms of past, present, and future. Its meaning is subject to constant change. The period of time we measure as a year passes relatively slowly for children and quickly for adults. The same unit of time passes slowly when we are waiting, particularly when associated with anxiety, and…

Historical and scriptural evidence affirms that the Saints at the time of Christ believed in a God who was a personal being, one who was both corporeal and anthropomorphic. This God of whom the scriptures speak spoke face to face with the prophet called to stand at the head of each major gospel dispensation. Adam, scripture attests, walked and talked with God in Eden. The heart of Job's dilemma is that there is only one God. He has been asked by the friends to reconcile the all too obvious evil in creation with his confidence in a God who will punish evil. The Job dialogue thus represents the struggles of a man coming to terms with monotheism, and being deprived of the more ancient polytheistic view.169 Compare this with Bruce Pritchett's discussion of Job in…
